broker_fee_detail: FARE Act notice shown. Listed by NYC Luxury Apt Rentals; confirm no tenant-paid/co-op sublet fee.
broker_message: Hi Arman, I'm interested in 1800 Ocean Parkway #C4. Can you tell me the floor and square footage, and confirm whether it's rent-stabilized (with rent history)? I noticed the unit was listed and delisted in January and see city records of recurring heat/hot-water complaints and elevator violations — how have those been addressed? Given comparable 1-beds nearby around $1,800, would the owner do $1,800 or a month free? Happy to tour the exact unit.
broker_questions: Which floor is C4 and what is the square footage?
Why was the January 2026 listing delisted after a week?
The building has recurring heat/hot-water complaints — how has heat performed?
Are the elevator fireman-service/recall issues resolved?
What is the 1st-floor medical office and any open DOB enforcement?
Is it rent-stabilized, with rent history?
Any tenant-paid/co-op sublet fee, and total move-in costs?
brokerage: NYC Luxury Apt Rentals Co LLC
building_risk_rationale: Augrented shows only 5 HPD violations (at/below Gravesend avg 7.6) and clean bedbugs, BUT flags a 97% 12-month severe-failure forecast and recent recurring problems: repeated building-wide Heat/Hot-Water complaints (Nov 2024 Class C 'no heat'; scalding-hot-water Class C Mar 2026; more May 2026), Sept 2024 rodent, July 2024 ceiling leak, and multiple ACTIVE ECB penalties (inoperative lobby fireman elevator recall ~$1,250; failure-to-maintain $655; unpermitted 1st-floor medical-office conversion contrary to C of O). Low raw count but a genuine recurring-heat + elevator-safety + illegal-conversion pattern pushes to Medium. Exact current OPEN count behind paywall (unverified).
commute_notes: Closest is the F at Kings Highway (Culver), a short flat ~6-min walk. F to Midtown ~55-65 min — a long single-seat ride. StreetEasy table garbled; the N does not stop here. Long commute to Manhattan.
concessions_to_ask: Written heat/hot-water and elevator repair assurances
cons: Recurring building-wide heat/hot-water complaints incl. a Class C no-heat
Active elevator fireman-service and unpermitted-conversion violations; 97% maintenance-risk forecast
Long F-train commute; thin listing with relist churn
leverage_notes: History is the leverage: the same $1,899 listing was pulled after a week in January and is back; comps sit at $1,800-$1,975. Recurring heat and active elevator/illegal-conversion violations are legitimate points to press. A $1,800 ask or a free month is defensible.
noise_risks: Ocean Parkway is a wide arterial with a service road; front units get traffic noise
owner_entity: 1800 Ocean Parkway Corp. (registered); managed by Ocean Parkway Management LLC
plain_english: A fee-free, fairly priced 1-bed near the F, but the building record is the catch: low raw violation count yet recurring heat/hot-water failures, active elevator-safety fines, and an unpermitted 1st-floor medical office. Only 6 days old (not stale), but the same listing was pulled after a week in January, which gives negotiating room. Tour only if you can get a rent-stabilized confirmation, heat/elevator assurances, and ideally $1,800 or a free month.
pros: No fee, 1-bed at a fair ~$1,899 with elevator and live-in super
Short flat walk to the F at Kings Highway

red_flags.how_to_verify: Ask why the earlier listing was pulled and whether it rented
red_flags.issue: Relisted after prior delist; rent frozen at $1,899 since Jan 2026 (up from $1,749 in 2024)
red_flags.why: Listed Jan 6, delisted Jan 13, relisted Jun 25 — churn suggests soft demand

red_flags.how_to_verify: Ask about heat reliability; check HPD Online; talk to a tenant
red_flags.why: Multiple emergency complaints 2024-2026 incl. a Class C 'no heat' — winter habitability risk

red_flags.how_to_verify: Ask when elevator was last inspected/repaired; check DOB records
red_flags.issue: Active elevator safety ECB violations (fireman-service recall inoperative)
red_flags.why: Fire-safety defect in an elevator building

red_flags.how_to_verify: Ask about the commercial tenant and any DOB enforcement
red_flags.issue: Unpermitted 1st-floor medical-office conversion contrary to C of O
red_flags.why: Active ECB violations; can bring inspectors and enforcement

red_flags.how_to_verify: Insist on an in-person tour and floor plan/sqft
red_flags.issue: Only 4 photos, no floor/sqft, text-only contact, hype ('WILL NOT LAST')
red_flags.why: Thin listing and pressure language reduce transparency

unverified: exact current open HPD violation count
